sanskrit: बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् । स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥
hindiTranslation: बाह्यस्पर्शमें आसक्तिरहित अन्तःकरणवाला साधक आत्मामें जो सुख है, उसको प्राप्त होता है ।  फिर वह ब्रह्ममें अभिन्नभावसे स्थित मनुष्य अक्षय सुखका अनुभव करता है ॥
englishTranslation: One who is not attached to outer contacts finds the joy within the true self. Unified with the supreme reality, that person enjoys unending happiness.
essence: Freedom begins when outer contact stops controlling inner joy.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Real happiness is found by turning away from outside pleasures and resting in the true self. That inner joy does not wear out.
simpleInsight: Relief comes from knowing lasting contentment is not dependent on changing circumstances.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna teaches the way beyond restless desire. After describing steadiness and equanimity, Krishna says the one who is no longer hooked by outer contact discovers joy within and enters an unending peace.
modernRelevance: You close the app, the room goes quiet, and the buzz is still there in your chest. No amount of scrolling, praise, or distraction reaches the place that actually needs rest.
translationLiteral: बाह्यस्पर्शेषु (in outer contacts) / असक्त-आत्मा (one whose inner being is not attached) / विन्दति (finds) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / यत् (which) / सुखम् (happiness) / सः (that one) / ब्रह्म-योग-युक्त-आत्मा (one whose inner being is joined in union with the supreme reality) / सुखम् (happiness) / अक्षयम् (imperishable) / अश्नुते (enjoys, attains)
